title: Yosys assertion error, rtlil.cc:3277
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Using the attached files, run "read -formal test.v".  Yosys then crashes before a next command can be issued.

## Expected behavior

1. Minimum: I would like to see an error message indicating what line of my file is broken
2. Because of the `default_nettype none line, I would expect to see an error before yosys attempts to elaborate using an unknown identifier.

## Actual behavior

    yosys> read -formal test.v
    1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend.
    Parsing formal SystemVerilog input from `test.v' to AST representation.
    Generating RTLIL representation for module `\test'.
    ERROR: Assert `chunk.width >= 0' failed in kernel/rtlil.cc:3277.

This appears to be related to [this other issue](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/issues/579), but the example test looks quite different.

Here's the test.v file.

[test.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/2881326/test.zip)

Dan
